data.q: What does RUM stand for in web observability?
data.e: RUM stands for Real User Monitoring, which involves collecting data from actual user interactions on a web application.

data.q: Which of the following is a primary goal of Real User Monitoring (RUM)?
o: Capturing real user experiences in production
data.e: RUM captures data from actual users in production to understand real-world performance and usage.

data.q: Which key metric is typically captured by RUM to measure page load performance?
data.e: Time to First Byte (TTFB) is a common metric in RUM indicating how quickly the server responds with data.

data.q: Which JavaScript API is commonly used by RUM tools to capture detailed timing information about page loads?
data.e: The Performance API provides detailed timing info such as navigation start, DOM content loaded, and load events.

data.q: What is a common way RUM collects data from real users?
o: Embedding lightweight JavaScript in the web page
data.e: RUM typically uses lightweight JavaScript snippets embedded in pages to send telemetry back to servers.

data.q: In the context of frontend RUM, what does the 'Largest Contentful Paint' (LCP) measure?
o: Time taken to load the largest visible element
Time taken to fully load the page
data.e: LCP measures how long it takes for the largest visible content element (e.g., image or text block) to render.

data.q: Why is Real User Monitoring preferred over synthetic monitoring for frontend observability?
o: It captures real user experiences including network and device variations
data.e: RUM captures performance under actual user conditions, reflecting diverse devices, networks, and user interactions.

data.q: Which of the following performance metrics can RUM capture on the client side?
o: Page load time, resource load timings, user interactions timing
data.e: RUM collects client-side metrics like page load and user interaction timings but not server internal stats.

data.q: What role does the Navigation Timing API play in Real User Monitoring?
o: Provides timestamps related to various stages of page loading
Measures memory usage of the application
data.e: The Navigation Timing API exposes detailed timing data for the page load lifecycle useful in RUM.

data.q: Which of these is NOT typically captured by frontend RUM?
data.e: User's CPU temperature is not captured; RUM focuses on user experience metrics and error tracking.
